description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a coarse, granular texture and a predominantly brown coloration interspersed with white and gray mineral streaks. The surface is uneven, with jagged edges and rough patches, suggesting it is a natural stone rather than a cut or polished specimen. One side of the rock features a small, circular label or marking, possibly for identification or cataloging purposes. The rock's coloration and mineral composition indicate it could be granite or a similar igneous rock, commonly found in outdoor urban landscaping or as a natural barrier. Its size and mass make it suitable for use as an obstacle or decorative element in parks, sidewalks, or garden beds. The rock's hard, rough surface would make it difficult to move without significant effort, and it is not shaped to serve as a receptacle or seating surface.
description: A medium-sized, rough-textured brown rock with white and gray mineral streaks and an irregular, jagged shape. It features a small circular label on one side and appears solid and heavy.
description_view_0: The rock is seen from a broad angle, showing its rough, brown surface with white streaks and a visible circular label on the upper right.
description_view_1: A narrow side view highlights the rock's thickness and vertical profile, with the label partially visible at the top edge.
description_view_2: This view displays the widest, flattest face of the rock, emphasizing its coarse texture and mottled brown coloration.
description_view_3: A side profile reveals the rock's height and the transition from brown to white mineral deposits along the base.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
